stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • "Longplayer" is a musical composition by experimental musician Jem Finer.
  • A specially designed computer system at Trinity Buoy Wharf in London, England, began playing the piece in 2000.
  • It is scheduled to stop playing the piece in 3000.
  • The performance will last 1,000 years.
  • It will be among the longest continuous musical performances ever planned.
stem: The student wants to indicate how long Jem Finer's musical piece will last. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Lasting 1,000 years, Jem Finer's musical piece will be among the longest continuous musical performances ever planned.
Jem Finer is the composer of the musical piece "Longplayer."
"Longplayer" is a musical piece currently being played at Trinity Buoy Wharf in London, England.
A computer system at Trinity Buoy Wharf in London began playing a musical piece by experimental musician Jem Finer.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence indicates how long Jem Finer's musical piece will last, noting that it will last 1,000 years and be among the longest continuous musical performances ever planned.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence identifies Jem Finer as the composer and provides the name of the musical piece; it doesn't indicate how long the piece will last. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence provides the name of the musical piece and where it is currently being played; it doesn't indicate how long the piece will last. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence mentions where the musical piece is being played and identifies the composer; it doesn't indicate how long the piece will last.
